module_map.responsibility: Main orchestrator: coordinates fetch (metadata collection), sort, and display pipeline
File metadata abstraction: collects, aggregates, and caches filesystem attributes from paths. Central data structure passed through the pipeline
key_types: Meta (9M, main struct carrying all file attributes)
key_functions: from_path (106L: creates Meta from Path; core of metadata pipeline)
recurse_into (127L: recursive directory traversal with caching)

module_map.responsibility: Renders Meta objects to terminal output with formatting, alignment, tree structure, colors, icons, and git status
key_functions: tree (38L: entry point for tree mode)
inner_display_grid (116L: builds aligned grid with padding rules)
get_output (106L: converts single Meta to formatted output string per Block)

module_map.responsibility: CLI argument parsing, configuration serialization, feature toggles for display and behavior
submodules: blocks.rs (604L, 52F, 2C: Block enum defining display columns; configure_from/from_config)

module_map.responsibility: Sorting logic: implements all 18 sort orders (by name, size, date, extension, version, git status, etc.)
Color palette management: resolves file color based on lscolors (LS_COLORS env), theme selection, and file type
key_functions: get_color (90L: resolves color for file by type/extension)
to_content_style (60L: converts lscolors::Style to crossterm style)

module_map.responsibility: Theme definitions and icon mappings: resolves icons by file type and colors by context
Icon resolution facade: selects icon based on file type, name, extension; uses IconTheme
Git integration: caches and queries git status (untracked, staged, modified, ignored status per file)
Reads TOML config from ~/.config/lsd/config.yaml; maps to Flags and ColorTheme

data_flow.description: For each input Path, creates Meta struct containing: file name (meta/name.rs), type (meta/filetype.rs), size (meta/size.rs), permissions (meta/permissions.rs), owner (meta/owner.rs), modified date (meta/date.rs), git status (queried via git.rs), and metadata cache entry. Recursive via recurse_into for directories.

Receives Vec<Meta> from fetch, applies SortType (from flags) to reorder in-place. Sorts by name, size, date, extension, version, git status, or reverse of each. Mutates input; no type transformations.

During display phase, for each Meta, resolves color from Meta::name, filetype, and theme. Uses lscolors (LS_COLORS env), or theme-based fallback. No mutation; color is computed on-demand during rendering.
For each Meta, queries IconTheme to resolve icon string and width. Uses Meta::name, filetype, extension. Returns icon char and display width for alignment.

Core rendering function. For each Meta and each Block in display flags, renders a column: name (with tree prefix if tree mode), size, date, permissions, owner, git status, icons, colors. Uses padding_rules (computed from all Metas) for grid alignment. Calls into flags/ modules for each Block type.

Assembles get_output lines into final output. Computes padding_rules by scanning all Metas first (max width per Block). Grid mode: simple left-aligned columns. Tree mode: adds tree prefixes (├── │  └──) based on depth and position. Concatenates with newlines.

cross_module_hubs.reason: Central data hub. Inbound from: core.rs (fetch), display.rs (for rendering), icon.rs (for icon resolution), git.rs (for git status), theme/ (indirectly). Outbound to: meta/* submodules (name, filetype, permissions, owner, date, size, access_control, symlink, windows_*). All file attributes flow through Meta struct.

Configuration hub. Inbound from: core.rs (new), app.rs (CLI parsing), config_file.rs (config loading). Outbound: used everywhere (display.rs, sort.rs, core.rs, icon.rs, theme/color.rs). All feature toggles and display options originate here.

Output rendering hub. Inbound from: core.rs (display). Outbound to: flags/blocks (Block enum, to_string), color.rs (Colors::get_color), icon.rs (Icon::from_path), git_theme.rs (git status colors), sort.rs indirectly (sorted metas). Orchestrates final rendering across all display concerns.

files_to_modify: src/flags/blocks.rs (add Checksum variant to Block enum)
src/meta/mod.rs (add checksum field to Meta struct)
new_types: Meta.checksum: Option<String> (hex-encoded SHA-256 hash, populated on-demand)
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Existing pattern: Similar to owner (meta/owner.rs) and permissions (meta/permissions.rs). (1) Add field to Meta struct. (2) Create meta/checksum.rs with from_path or lazy computation. (3) Add Block variant. (4) In display.rs get_output, add match arm for Block::Checksum to render the checksum field. (5) Add CLI flag via flags/display.rs. (6) Integrate into padding_rules computation for grid alignment.
change_proposal.integration_point: Block<Checksum> inserted into Blocks::configure_from (flags/blocks.rs line 104+) alongside other optional blocks. In display.rs get_output (line 325+), add Block::Checksum case to render meta.checksum with appropriate truncation/padding. Checksum computation triggered in Meta::from_path (meta/mod.rs line 261+) when ChecksumOption flag is set, or lazy-loaded on first display access to avoid I/O on every file.
risks: Performance: Computing SHA-256 for every file (large files, recursive dirs) adds I/O and CPU. Mitigation: Make optional (off by default), cache results per session, warn if used with recursive deep traversals.
Unicode display width: SHA-256 hex string is 64 chars, fixed width. No issue, but ensure get_visible_width (display.rs line 555) treats it as 64 displayable chars.
Windows compat: std::fs::read / sha2 crate are portable. No platform-specific issues.
Sorting: If user wants --sort checksum, requires adding new SortType variant in sort.rs and comparison logic. Not in scope of initial implementation.
Config file: If config_file.rs unmarshals checksum: bool from TOML, ensure serde defaults to false to avoid breaking existing configs.
